titleStmt.title: SII 36.6: original edition by P Shanmugam
No. 6. (A.R. No. 6 of 1910.) MUḌIGOṆḌAM (Muḍigoṇḍa), KOLLEGAL TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT. On the south wall of the Lakshmī Nārāyaṇaśvāmi temple.

summary.p: (Viḷambi, Chittirai, śu., 3, Sunday, Rōhiṇi = A.D. 1238, April 18, Sunday).

Two merchants from Muḍikoṇḍam purchased a piece of land measuring 750 kuḻi, and granted to Dēsip-perumāḷ as tax free (iṟaiyili) tiruviḍaiyāṭṭam for the supply of sacred food offerings. The four Aḍaippu Mudalis of Koḷḷagar (Koḷḷēgāl) probably received the grant for maintenance. The inscription curses those who are against the purport of the grant as rājadrōhis and enemies of the country (nāṭṭup-pakaiyan). At the end of the record the following five symbols, representing mercantile guild are engraved: drum, dagger, anvil, moon and sun.
Published in E.C. (n), Vol. IV, No. Kol. 94.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svastiśrī pocaḷa śrī vīrasomīśvaratevan prathvirācciyam paṇṇi Aruḷānikka viḷampi samvaṟsarattu cittirai māsamum pūrvvapakṣamun trutiyaiyun nāyaṟṟuk kiḻamaiyum peṟṟa Urocaṇi nāḷ
koḷḷakaraṭaippu mutalikaḷ kotai Āḻvānum Eṭuttakaiyaḻakiyānuñ caṅkattaḻakanu muyakkoṇṭapiḷḷaiyum Ivvanaivar pakkalu muṭikoṇṭattil tecip perumāḷukkut tiruviṭaiyāṭṭamāka
Im muṭikoṇṭattil viyāpārikaḷil kirantaimuḷḷaār Ēṟāṇṭaiyāna Aḻakiyamaṇavāḷadāsaruṅ kirantaiperumāḷ mātāṇṭaiyuṅ koṅkaṇakaṉ Āḻvānmāruñ caṅkattaḻakan
mārumivar pakkal poṉṉaṟak kuṭuttu maṇṇaṟak koṇṭu viṭṭa kuḻi 750 m Amutupaṭikku cantrātittavarai Iṟaiyili Itu Āhipiṭittavan rājaderāhi nāṭṭup pakaiyan va derāhi
Digital edition of SII 36.6 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
